$comment: USML 2026.05 composition graph example. Non-normative; demonstrates the reverse-lineage relationships between three patterns. The graph shape itself is not a validated USML data-model object — it's a documentation artifact showing how peerPacks references resolve across the catalog.

edges.consumedVia: hero-eyebrow slot uses prim-label when an eyebrow badge is rendered
hero-background slot composes decoration-overlay for grain/texture effects
all slots consume token references resolved against tokens-baseline
all button-system slots consume token references

notes: v3.G.17 enforces composition reality: where pattern-hero declares peerPacks to pattern-button-system, the hero's specimen MUST consume button-system through real composition (class='action-button'), not by re-implementing button styling.

v3.G.21 enforces optionalPeerPacks semantics: prim-label is consumed only by hero's type-only variant (for eyebrow badges), and prim-decoration-overlay is consumed only by gradient-mesh/brand-photo/video variants. Other variants must not be flagged for missing composition.
Reverse-lineage discipline (D.82) requires every consumed peer pack's README to list its consumers. The graph above is the canonical source of truth for those README tables.
